mission.title: opentofu: Dynamic provider configuration assignment
mission.description: Dynamic provider configuration assignment. Requested by 280+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
helm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu
```
Suggest an existing issue in legacy Terraform to fix in OpenTF. 

To increase automation we struggle many times when configuring providers.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Dynamic provider configuration assignment
Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Dynamic provider configuration assignment" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: There is a tricky situation that I don't see discussed in [the current RFC](https://github.com/opentofu/opentofu/blob/0cc4301cd51bf3ef1de4d87edfef2ab81cb678b8/rfc/20240513-static-evaluation-providers.md).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentofu installed or the issue environment reproducible.
